alt_test_caveat: alt-test ω is a MODEL-JURY BOOTSTRAP (a documented director decision). The reference 'annotators' are the other panel models, drawn from the SAME population being seated, so ω is CIRCULAR. A valid alt-test needs >=3 HUMAN annotators on >=30 items (Calderon, Reichart & Dror 2025, arXiv:2501.10970). Here ω functions as an OUTLIER DETECTOR (a judge that agrees with its peers no worse than peers agree with each other), NOT a substitution guarantee. Seat decisions are PROVISIONAL until human labels exist (§12 Q3).

kappa_baseline.source: hardcoded default — NOT human-estimated; the κ one-sided floor and κ-z gates (which can SEAT or REJECT a judge) are PROVISIONAL until --human-labels supplies a measured human–human Krippendorff α (§12 Q3)

notes: accuracy (difficulty-weighted) 0.985 (≥ target); Wilson-95 lower 0.935 > 0.50 → real margin over chance ✓
agreement r=0.979 (≥ 0.80); κ=0.978 (≥ one-sided floor 0.719); z vs human +4.30 (SUPER-consistent → review-flag (Tier-1B, SEATED))

review_flag SET — κ=0.978 sits z=+4.30 above the human baseline (Han 2025 Tier-1B: super-consistent judges are TOP-ranked and SEATED). Flagged for later human review only IF κ≈1.0 co-occurs with high human disagreement on the same items — NOT screened.
alt-test ω=0.667 [model-jury PROXY (CIRCULAR; not a substitution guarantee)] (≥ 0.50 → seat-eligible)
consistency 1.000 (≥ 0.80 test-retest floor)
